On 10 November, the Securities Commission of the Bahamas froze the assets of one of FTX's subsidiaries, FTX Digital Markets Ltd, "and related parties", and provisionally appointed an attorney as liquidator.
I have supervised situations involving novel financial structures (Enron and Residential Capital) and cross-border asset recovery and maximization (Nortel and Overseas Shipholding)…
Despite FTX's bankruptcy, Bankman-Fried continued to attempt to raise money for the firm during the weekend of 12 and 13 November.
The Financial Times reported that FTX's balance sheet shortly before the bankruptcy showed $9 billion in liabilities against $900 million in liquid assets, $5 billion in "less liquid" assets, and $3.2 billion in illiquid private equity investments.
Following the collapse of FTX, the Royal Bahamas Police Force launched a criminal investigation into the company.
Cryptocurrency investment firms with assets still held on FTX after its bankruptcy include Galois Capital and Galaxy Digital.

Blockchain analysts claim that about $650 million worth of cryptocurrency left the Bahamas-based digital asset exchange in the hack, making it one of the largest crypto attacks of 2022."},{"url":"https://www.pbs.org/newshour/economy/probe-underway-after-funds-vanish-from-bankrupt-crypto-exchange-ftx","title":"Probe underway after funds vanish from bankrupt crypto exchange FTX | PBS News","content":"NEW YORK (AP) — Collapsed cryptocurrency trading firm FTX confirmed there was "unauthorized access" to its accounts, hours after the company filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection Friday.
Exactly how much money is involved is unclear, but analytics firm Elliptic estimated Saturday that $477 million was missing from the exchange.
Another $186 million was moved out of FTX's accounts, but that may have been FTX moving assets to storage, said Elliptic's co-founder and chief scientist Tom Robinson.
The company had valued its assets between $10 billion to $50 billion, and listed more than 130 affiliated companies around the world, according to its bankruptcy filing."},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/video/2022/12/28/doj-investigating-how-372-million-vanished-in-hack-after-ftx-collapse.html","title":"DOJ investigating how $372 million vanished in hack after FTX collapse","content":"DOJ investigating how $372 million vanished in hack after FTX collapse"},{"url":"https://www.ropesgray.com/-/media/files/alerts/2026/07/20260714_crypto_quarterly.pdf","title":"Crypto Quarterly Table of Contents","date":"3 days ago","content":"3 days ago — On April 1, 2026, the DOJ—working in conjunction with the FBI and other law enforcement agencies—announced that it recovered over $600,000 ...Read more8 pages"}]}

FTX filed for bankruptcy protection after a crypto equivalent of a bank run. FTX, the world's second-largest cryptocurrency exchange, on Saturday said it was probing a possible hacking attack, a day after it collapsed when traders rushed to withdraw billions of dollars. The *Wall Street Journal (WSJ)* reported that more than $500 million (€482 million) of crypto funds appear to be missing, citing crypto analytics firm Elliptic Enterprises who said the funds had been moved out from the platform in \"suspicious circumstances.\". The potential hack took place just after FTX filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection on Friday, the company's general counsel Ryne Miller said in a tweet. John Ray, who was appointed on Friday as Chief Restructuring Officer and CEO of FTX following the removal of founder Sam Bankman-Fried, said \"unauthorized access to certain assets has occurred.\".
